question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing that the Consumer Price Index increased only 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March. This is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. Since the actual data shows 0.3% monthly increase versus the required >0.8%, the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March CPI increased 0.3% monthly
0.3% is well below the 0.8% threshold for YES resolution
The report was released on April 10, 2026 as scheduled
Data is final and official from the Bureau of Labor Statistics
risks: Potential data revisions (though historically rare for monthly CPI)
Market may not have fully processed the official release yet
